en: It's very hot today, isn't it?
Yes, it's so hot. It looks like summer is officially starting.
ko: 응, 너무 더워. 여름이 본격적으로 시작되나 봐.

en: I'll have to remember to buy some sunblock then. My name is John, by the way.
I'm Yumi. Nice to meet you.
Nice to meet you, too. Are you from around here?
ko: 나도 만나서 반가워. 이 근처 사는 거야?

en: No, I'm from Hokkaido in Japan. It's much colder than here, even in summer.
ko: 아니, 나는 일본 홋카이도에서 왔어. 여름에도 여기보다 훨씬 추워.

en: What's the weather like in your hometown at this time of year?
I'm from London. It rains a lot there, so I'm not used to these summer temperatures.
ko: 나는 런던 출신이야. 거기는 비가 많이 와서 이런 여름 더위에 익숙하지 않아.

en: Oh, really? I like summer because we can go and spend time at the beach.
ko: 아, 정말? 나는 여름이 좋아. 해변에 가서 시간 보낼 수 있으니까.

en: I take my dog every time. He loves to run into the sea.
ko: 매번 강아지를 데려가. 바다로 뛰어드는 걸 좋아해.

en: What do you normally do in the summer in London?
If it's sunny, we often meet up and have picnics in the park.
ko: 날씨가 좋으면 만나서 공원에서 피크닉을 자주 해.

en: London has some really nice parks. Have you ever been there?
ko: 런던에는 정말 멋진 공원이 많아. 가 본 적 있어?

en: I've never been there, but I've always wanted to visit the museums.
ko: 가 본 적은 없는데 항상 박물관에 가보고 싶었어.

en: What do people do in their free time there?
ko: 거기 사람들은 여가 시간에 뭘 해?

en: People often go to watch football games at the weekend.
ko: 주말에 축구 경기 보러 자주 가.

en: Everyone is very passionate about their favorite team.
ko: 모두가 자기 좋아하는 팀에 대해 엄청 열정적이야.

en: That sounds like baseball in Japan. It's super popular.
ko: 일본에서 야구가 그런 것 같아. 엄청 인기 있어.
